siteSettings.footerNote: Soulful Algarve creates slower, brighter retreats across the Portuguese coast with a warm pink-and-beige aesthetic.

seo.title: Soulful Algarve | Retreats in Portugal
seo.description: Design-led retreats in Portugal for small groups, private escapes, and founder offsites, with slow days, coastal light, and beautifully hosted evenings.

hero.eyebrow: Portugal retreats for slower, brighter days
hero.bodyLead: Retreats shaped for rest, creativity, and small-group connection along the Portuguese coast.
hero.description: Soulful Algarve hosts design-led retreats in Portugal for guests who want a softer pace, a stronger sense of place, and long-table evenings that feel restorative rather than scheduled.
meta: Yoga, surf, design, and slow dining

experienceSection.description: Every stay is built around light structure, warm service, and enough quiet to let the setting do some of the work. Nothing feels generic and nothing feels overfilled.

cards.title: Retreat design with a softer pace
cards.description: Each stay is built around sunrise movement, deep work or reflection blocks, long lunches, and evenings that feel social rather than scheduled.
points: Hosts handle transport and timing from arrival to final transfer.
Every retreat includes generous downtime by the pool, beach, or garden.

cards.title: Portugal as atmosphere, not backdrop
cards.description: We use restored estates, coastal villas, and low-key design hotels that feel grounded in place instead of generic wellness venues.
points: Sintra forests for cool mornings and candlelit dinners.
Comporta and Algarve settings for salt air, open sky, and warm late light.

cards.title: For private groups and open dates
cards.description: Book a scheduled retreat or shape a private edition for founders, creative teams, or friend groups who want a better format than the usual offsite.
points: Optional surf coaching, ceramics, sound sessions, and chef-led suppers.
Planning support for workshops, celebrations, and brand moments.

retreatsSection.description: Open dates work for individual guests and pairs. Private editions work for teams, founder groups, and creative circles who want a more memorable setting than a standard offsite.

cards.label: Signature
cards.title: Atlantic Reset
cards.description: A five-night coastal retreat in Comporta for guests who want movement, sea air, and unhurried evenings.

cards.label: Private Groups
cards.title: Founders by the Sea
cards.description: A warm, design-led offsite for small teams who want strategy sessions without losing the softness of a retreat.

cards.label: Creative Escape
cards.title: Pink Evening Residency
cards.description: For writers, designers, and artists who want studio time, hosted meals, and a setting that resets the nervous system.

items.title: Terrace movement
items.description: Pilates, yoga, or breath-led mobility on a shaded terrace while the coast is still quiet.

items.title: Slow breakfast
items.description: Fruit, local pastries, coffee, and a briefing for the day served family-style in the garden.

items.title: Core session
items.description: Choose between guided workshops, surf, ceramics, or unstructured time for reading and rest.

items.title: Golden-hour reset
items.description: Poolside pause, beach walk, or sauna before dinner starts to gather everyone back together.

items.title: Long-table dinner
items.description: Seasonal Portuguese menus, natural wine, and one hosted conversation or live acoustic set.

itinerarySection.sideTitle: Every edition keeps the same emotional rhythm
itinerarySection.sideDescription: Quiet mornings, one central experience, a generous late afternoon, and dinner that feels like the social heart of the day.
sidePoints: Airport transfers and local movement are handled for the group.
Chef-led menus center local produce, seafood, and relaxed service.
Optional add-ons bring in surf, ceramics, sound, or strategy facilitation.

portugalSection.description: Portugal makes retreat logistics cleaner and the atmosphere warmer. It is one of the few places where design, weather, food, and access all pull in the same direction.

notes.title: Direct flights, easy arrivals
notes.description: Lisbon and Faro keep travel days light for international guests, especially private groups arriving from multiple cities.

notes.title: The shoulder season is still golden
notes.description: Portugal gives us dependable light, outdoor dining, and warm evenings without the compressed feeling of peak summer.

notes.title: The local network is excellent
notes.description: Chefs, ceramics studios, surf instructors, and villa hosts make the experience feel specific to place instead of imported.

testimonialsSection.headingScript: lighter, clearer, and a little sun-flushed

items.quote: It felt like a beautifully designed exhale. We had structure when we wanted it and space when we needed it.
items.author: Mara V., brand strategist

items.quote: The venue, the food, and the pace were all sharper than a typical wellness retreat. Nothing felt generic.
items.author: Elin R., creative director

items.quote: We used the private format for a leadership offsite and left with clearer thinking than any hotel conference setup has ever given us.
items.author: Rui P., startup founder

items.quote: Portugal was the right call. Warm, calm, and beautiful without trying too hard.
items.author: Tess L., repeat guest

items.question: Who are these retreats for?
items.answer: They are designed for solo guests, couples, friends, and private groups who want beautiful logistics, thoughtful programming, and enough breathing room to actually feel restored.

items.question: Are flights included?
items.answer: Flights are not included. We provide arrival guidance, transfer coordination, and clear timing so getting in from Lisbon or Faro is straightforward.

items.question: Can this become a private company offsite?
items.answer: Yes. The private formats are built for small teams that want a strategic offsite with better energy, better meals, and more intentional pacing.

items.question: Do I need retreat experience?
items.answer: No. The structure is welcoming to first-time guests. There is always a balance between guided sessions and optional downtime.

contactSection.headingScript: with dates, venue mood, and guest count in mind
contactSection.description: Share whether you want an open retreat place, a private group stay, or a team offsite, and we can guide you toward the right location, season, and rhythm.
contactSection.panelDescription: Tell us your ideal month, guest count, and the balance you want between rest, movement, and work.
contactSection.listTitle: What to send in your enquiry
listItems: The balance you want between rest, activity, and work
contactSection.smallPrint: Online editing is powered by TinaCMS, so your team can update this site through the Tina sidebar and inline editor once the Tina Cloud project is connected.
